query: I already ran two compat-finder rounds against 12 extension builds and got `issue, pass`. Which versions should I test next?
Can you explain whether I should use compat-finder `interactive` or `next` here? I want a guided CLI flow for narrowing a bad plugin release.
I'm adding compat-finder to an internal troubleshooting page. Show me the normal TypeScript session API flow and when I would drop to the lower-level state helpers.
compat-finder next gave me `{ "status": "testing", "targets": ["beta-banner", "new-checkout"] }`. What do those targets mean and how should I report the result back?
I want to try compat-finder without installing it into this repo. What's the quickest `npx` command to confirm the CLI is available?
Help me wire compat-finder into a custom React admin tool so support engineers can continue a saved troubleshooting session after refresh.
I have a failing regression between two git commits. Tell me which commit to test next and whether I should use git bisect.
Plan a QA matrix for five feature flags across three browsers. I need a practical test order, but I'm not using compat-finder or any package for this.
My login test fails only in production. Help me troubleshoot the service logs and deployment config.
Should I store session state in localStorage or Redis for my app's wizard flow? There's no compat-finder logic involved, just normal product state.
What's the next thing I should test for this flaky Playwright spec? I don't have pass/issue answers yet and I'm not using compat-finder.
Build me a command-line wizard that asks users questions and narrows causes over time. This is a brand new tool, not an integration with compat-finder.
